summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a crafted element name injects a second XML element into the serialized output, and the injection persists even when requireWellFormed=true is requested.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should create an element with the crafted tag name 'foo/><evil attr="1"', serialize the document in default and requireWellFormed modes, and show that both outputs contain an injected <evil/> sibling element that survives reparsing.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit 08a22d78e4bc50f12ce9f5090b8d96ee6031ac7b, built the reproduction image, and then printed '[evohunt] default=<root><foo/><evil attr="1"/></root>', '[evohunt] requireWellFormed=<root><foo/><evil attr="1"/></root>', 'default_evil_count=1', and 'strict_evil_count=1'. The verifier ended with '[evohunt] verification confirmed injected XML nodes via unchecked element names' and run.sh exited successfully.
verification_method: The exploit constructs a document with createElement('foo/><evil attr="1"'), serializes it in both default and requireWellFormed modes, reparses both outputs, and asserts that each contains exactly one injected <evil/> element. Matching outputs across both modes proves the serializer emits the unchecked tag name verbatim instead of enforcing well-formed element-name checks.
evidence_produced_by_run: The cloned target at commit 08a22d78e4bc50f12ce9f5090b8d96ee6031ac7b serialized the crafted element name to '<root><foo/><evil attr="1"/></root>'.
Reparsing the emitted XML showed one real injected <evil/> element in both default and requireWellFormed serializer outputs.
The verifier printed the expected success marker after confirming the injected element counts.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
